mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of introducing static methods in interfaces in Java 8?

options.text: To define abstract methods
To define utility methods related to the interface
To define instance methods
To define default methods

mcqs.explanation: Java 8 introduced static methods in interfaces to provide a way to encapsulate utility methods related to the interface, promoting cleaner and more cohesive code design.

mcqs.question: How can you call a static method in an interface?

options.text: By creating an instance of the interface
By using the interface name
By using the class name
By using the object reference

mcqs.explanation: Static methods in interfaces can be called directly using the interface name, without creating an instance of the interface.

mcqs.question: Can static methods in interfaces be overridden by classes that implement the interface?

options.text: Yes
No
Only if the class is abstract
Only if the class is final

mcqs.explanation: Static methods in interfaces cannot be overridden by classes that implement the interface, as they are implicitly public and belong to the interface itself.

mcqs.question: What is the main difference between static methods and default methods in interfaces?

options.text: Static methods are used for utility functions, while default methods provide a default implementation
Static methods are used for instance methods, while default methods provide a default implementation
Static methods are used for abstract methods, while default methods provide a default implementation
Static methods are used for default behavior, while default methods provide a default implementation

mcqs.explanation: Static methods in interfaces are used to provide utility functions related to the interface, while default methods provide a default implementation for classes that implement the interface.

mcqs.question: Can static methods in interfaces be used to define instance methods?

options.text: Yes
No
Only if the instance method is abstract
Only if the instance method is final

mcqs.explanation: Static methods in interfaces cannot be used to define instance methods, as they belong to the interface itself and do not have access to instance state.

mcqs.question: What is the benefit of using static methods in interfaces compared to traditional utility classes?

options.text: Static methods in interfaces provide a way to encapsulate utility methods related to the interface
Static methods in interfaces provide a way to define instance methods
Static methods in interfaces provide a way to define default behavior
Static methods in interfaces provide a way to define abstract methods

mcqs.explanation: Static methods in interfaces provide a way to encapsulate utility methods related to the interface, promoting cleaner and more cohesive code design.

mcqs.question: How do you define a static method in an interface?

options.text: Using the `static` keyword and providing a method body
Using the `abstract` keyword and providing a method body
Using the `default` keyword and providing a method body
Using the `final` keyword and providing a method body

mcqs.explanation: To define a static method in an interface, you use the `static` keyword and provide a method body.
